mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and consumer spending power, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive short-term equity market momentum within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of months with payroll increases exceeding 100k, or rolling IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in retail sales signals robust consumer demand that exceeds baseline expectations, prompting equity market participants to revise earnings forecasts upward and bid up S&P 500 prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when retail sales growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal fails to predict direction in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing the 4.5% threshold indicates labor market deterioration and weakening aggregate demand, which forces equity valuations lower as investors price in reduced corporate profitability over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of periods where unemployment exceeds 4.5%, or the negative predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of falling interest rates and rising inflation creates a real rate compression that boosts nominal equity valuations, as lower discount rates outweigh inflation-driven margin pressures over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or forward returns turn negative when both conditions coincide.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms a synchronized economic upswing, reducing recession probabilities and triggering rapid short-term equity market rallies within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when both series expand concurrently, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal a contraction in the construction sector and broader credit tightening, which drags down capital goods demand and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during credit-constrained environments.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines coincide with positive 60-day equity returns in >60% of observations, or the negative relationship disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress corporate margins and drive negative equity market performance over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods yield positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.
